task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser showing the SEB Server dashboard or interface, indicating a successful login (e.g., logged in as super-admin).

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Exam Configurations section
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SEB Server interface showing the list of Exam Configurations or the configuration management menu selected.

task_completion.description: Open or create the 'CS302 Web Technologies' configuration
task_completion.visual_evidence: The configuration editor screen is open with the title or name field explicitly showing 'CS302 Web Technologies'.
A checkbox, toggle, or dropdown for 'Web Inspector' (or 'Developer Console') is visibly checked or set to enabled within the configuration settings.

task_completion.description: Enable the Context Menu (Right Mouse Click) setting
task_completion.visual_evidence: A checkbox, toggle, or dropdown for 'Context Menu' or 'Right Mouse Click' is visibly checked or set to enabled within the configuration settings.
The agent clicking a 'Save' or 'Apply' button, or a success notification/toast indicating the configuration was successfully saved.

integrity.description: Agent used the web browser GUI to configure the server
integrity.visual_evidence: The actions are performed in a web browser window showing the SEB Server UI, rather than using terminal commands like curl or API scripts.

integrity.description: Agent did not bypass the UI by directly modifying the database
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows showing MariaDB/MySQL prompts or SQL UPDATE commands being executed.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual application hosted at localhost:8080
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar should show 'localhost:8080' or the appropriate local SEB Server URL, not a local HTML file or text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target configuration is named 'CS302 Web Technologies'. The UI settings to be enabled correspond to the internal database flags 'allowDeveloperConsole' (Web Inspector) and 'enableRightMouse' (Context Menu). Look for these specific UI equivalents being toggled to an active/enabled state.
